description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a double-wing structure, painted primarily in teal blue with roundel insignias on the wings and a tricolor rudder. The fuselage is streamlined and features a single propeller at the nose, with a visible open cockpit situated just behind the upper wing. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The wings are braced with struts and wires, giving the aircraft a classic early 20th-century military appearance. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with the rudder painted in blue, white, and red stripes. The overall design suggests a lightweight, maneuverable aircraft typical of World War I-era reconnaissance or fighter planes.
description: A vintage teal blue biplane with double wings, roundel insignias, a single propeller, and an open cockpit. The aircraft features fixed landing gear and a tricolor rudder.
description_view_0: The biplane is seen from a rear oblique angle, highlighting the upper wing, roundel insignias, and the tricolor rudder at the tail.
description_view_1: A direct rear view shows the full span of the double wings, the tail assembly, and the fixed landing gear beneath the fuselage.
description_view_2: The biplane is viewed from the opposite rear oblique, displaying the side of the fuselage, the propeller, and the cockpit area.
description_view_3: A direct front view reveals the single propeller, open cockpit, and the symmetrical structure of the double wings with visible struts.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; rudder features tricolor markings.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, likely single-seat with minimal canopy.
